perf.regime: One program per query token: streams KV in BLOCK_K chunks, computing the weighted ReLU MQA logits tile in registers (fp32), and maintains a running top-k candidate buffer (merged per chunk) — writes ONLY the selected [T, topk] int32 indices, never materializing the full [T, K] fp32 logits (issue #54). Memory-bound on the q[H,D] + k[K,D] reads; fuses logits + causal mask + top-k into one launch (the reference is two passes over K). num_warps=4.

notes: Fused streaming top-k (issue #54): computes weighted ReLU MQA logits tile-by-tile and keeps a running top-k candidate buffer, avoiding the [T,K] fp32 materialization + separate torch.topk launch of the unfused path. Verified on ds5 (GB10/sm_121, Triton 3.7.1): 6/7 sweep points pass element-wise verify (rtol=atol=0); 1 fp32+mask point reports correctness.passed=False due to the documented boundary discontinuity (einsum vs tl.dot fp32 accumulation order flips a near-tie — the selected SET matches, confirmed by tests/test_dsa_indexer_topk.py set-equality). TOPK compiled as tl.constexpr with tl.static_range (unrolled, matching topk_softmax_kernel — tl.range does not correctly accumulate the loop-carried selected mask). Masked positions use -1e30 sentinel (not -inf) so the iterative argmax can distinguish selected (-inf) from masked (-1e30) when topk > num_valid_positions (e.g. a causal window narrower than topk). MI300A perf + the headline memory-traffic win vs the unfused path (dsa_indexer_logits + torch.topk) is a beverin follow-up. BLOCK_K_TOTAL=next_pow2(K) so the full per-query logits row fits in one register buffer for Phase 2.
